title: Mode-switch handler ignores natural language phrasing
what_failed: Strict equality matcher accepted only the exact strings 'root mode' / 'switch to root mode' / 'go root'. Any natural variant fell through to the LLM, which then role-played the mode switch in text while the agent stayed in normie mode.
where_failed: pi_agent.py process_input() — the if cmd in (...) tuple match
why_likely: Command parsing was a tuple membership test; punctuation and surrounding words ('can u switch to root mode ?') made it miss.
reproduction: Start agent in normie mode. Send: 'can u switch to root mode ?'. Observe Pi produces a fake mode-switch dialogue but self.mode never changes; subsequent tool-requiring questions fail because there are no tools.
expected: Agent recognises mode-switch intent across natural phrasings and flips self.mode before any LLM call.
actual: Agent ignored the intent; LLM produced a fabricated mode-switch experience. Every subsequent claim of tool use during the rest of the session was a hallucination.
suggested_fix: Loose matcher: short message (<= 8 words after stripping punctuation) containing 'root mode' / 'normie mode' triggers the switch. Other commands stay strict.
